prose:
Fukui reactivity analysis of formaldehyde (H₂C=O) was performed using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (PySCF RKS, tier 'standard'), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1, with exact four-center integrals (no density fitting). Partial charges were computed via Mulliken population analysis. The N−1 (cation, charge +1, mult 2) and N+1 (anion, charge −1, mult 2) states were computed to construct the condensed Fukui functions.

Results per atom [O, C, H, H]:
  f+ (electrophilic attack): [0.46813824, 0.70839130, −0.08826939, −0.08826015]
  f− (nucleophilic attack):  [0.46742464, 0.11244422,  0.21007563,  0.21005551]
  f0 (radical attack):       [0.46778144, 0.41041776,  0.06090312,  0.06089768]
  Δf (dual descriptor):      [+0.00071360, +0.59594709, −0.29834503, −0.29831566]

Most electrophilic site: Carbon (atom 2), f+ = 0.70839130 (headline value reported).
Most nucleophilic site: Oxygen (atom 1), f− = 0.46742464.

The carbonyl carbon is confirmed as the dominant electrophilic center (large positive dual descriptor Δf = +0.596), consistent with known nucleophilic addition chemistry of formaldehyde. The carbonyl oxygen is the primary nucleophilic center.

Integrity: trustworthy = true, status = ok. All three integrity checks passed (f+ sum = 1.0000, f− sum = 1.0000, schema header present). No warnings reported — clean run.

CLI: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1
Output: formaldehyde_fukui_dft.json
Plot: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/formaldehyde/argo_claude-sonnet-4.6/20260704-053404_formaldehyde_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png
